label: Population (2024)
deltaNote: Growth rate ~1.5% annually; one of the world's youngest populations with median age ~22

label: Oil Production
deltaNote: Iraq is OPEC's second-largest producer; production growth constrained by OPEC+ quotas

label: Proven Oil Reserves
deltaNote: ~9% of world total; Iraq has barely explored its western desert, where further giant fields are suspected

label: GDP (Nominal)
source: World Bank, IMF Article IV Consultation 2024
deltaNote: Oil revenues account for ~90% of government budget and ~45% of GDP; non-oil economy remains underdeveloped

label: Internally Displaced Persons
source: IOM Iraq Displacement Tracking Matrix (DTM) Q3 2024
contestNote: Peak displacement was 6 million at height of ISIS conflict (2014–2017); remaining IDPs face return barriers in Sinjar, disputed territories, and Anbar Province

label: Years of Continuous Civilization
deltaNote: The world's oldest continuous urban civilization; includes the invention of writing, the wheel, and the first empires and law codes

label: ISIS Attacks in Iraq (2024)
contestNote: ISIS claimed responsibility for ~200 attacks; remainder attributed to ISIS by Iraqi security forces. ISIS defeated territorially (2017) but maintains active insurgency primarily in Anbar, Diyala, Kirkuk, and Salah al-Din provinces

label: Annual Oil Export Revenue
source: Iraqi Ministry of Oil, State Oil Marketing Organization (SOMO) 2024
deltaNote: 2022 record: $115 billion. Revenue fell with global oil price decline. Northern Kirkuk exports (~400K bpd) halted since March 2023 per arbitration ruling vs Turkey.
